headline: How French courts became Europe's toughest on women returning from IS
description: Of the 23 terrorism trials scheduled in French courts in 2026, 18 involve women who joined the Islamic State organization in Syria. While their role was once overlooked, they are now being judged on equal footing with men.

headline: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
'The current war between Iran and the US stems from decades of mutual humiliation'
Eating organic fruits and vegetables linked to lower risk of postmenopausal breast cancer
description: According to French research, for every daily 100-gram portion of conventionally grown fruits and vegetables replaced by their organic equivalents, the risk of developing postmenopausal breast cancer drops by 10%.

headline: HSBC charged for helping former Lebanese central bank head embezzle hundreds of millions
description: The Swiss subsidiary of HSBC is suspected of having helped former Lebanese central bank chief Riad Salameh embezzle more than $300 million from the country's central bank. The charges mark a significant development in the French judicial investigation.

headline: Artist Louise Vo Tan brings the Louvre's stolen crown jewels back to life
description: 'They were dazzling, they looked like a meringue,' said Vo Tan, who filmed the museum's treasures before they were stolen and is now displaying them in hologram form in the building once used for the Port-Coton foghorn.

headline: Bold new proposal calls for merging Paris with suburbs
description: Clément Beaune, the French high commissioner for strategy and planning, proposed a sweeping overhaul of the Greater Paris area and its local governments.

Marjane Satrapi: 'At age 10, I was training myself to become a political prisoner'
description: The Franco-Iranian artist, who gained worldwide recognition with the graphic novel 'Persepolis,' died at 56 years old, her family announced Thursday. Le Monde interviewed her in October 2020. We are republishing that interview today.

headline: Hungary's Magyar to Le Monde: 'I was not elected simply to change the government, but to change the regime'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, Viktor Orban's successor explains how he plans to recover the frozen €16 billion in European funds earmarked for Hungary. He insists that Budapest will not block the opening of EU accession talks for Ukraine, provided that Kyiv guarantees the rights of its Hungarian minority.

headline: Who is rich in France?
description: Is a childless couple wealthy if they earn €6,438 per month after taxes? A new report seeks to address the lack of set criteria for measuring this demographic.
